title: Personas
content: > **Canonical source:** [docs/prd/personas.md](../prd/personas.md)
> Persona definitions are product requirements, not architecture. This document
> summarises the architectural implications of the persona model.
## Persona Summary
AMPS serves eight personas across four authorization tiers. The persona model
drives three architectural decisions:
1. **Four authorization levels.** Platform Admin, Tenant Admin, Actions User,
and M2M Service map to distinct endpoint groups and policy enforcement.
See [Authorization Model](authorization.md).
2. **Three access channels.** Personas range from field workers on mobile
(via Guardian) to automated services (M2M). This requires Web, CLI, and MCP
interfaces — all backed by the same API with a shared Contracts project.
See [Architecture Overview](overview.md).
3. **Multi-language support.** International deployments with non-English field
workers require translation support on workflow definitions, compliance
profiles, escalation rules, and workflow states.
| Persona | Authorization Level | Primary Channel |
| P1: Field Inspector | Actions User | Guardian mobile (M2M) |
| P2: EHS Professional | Actions User | Web (Angular SPA) |
| P3: Compliance Officer / Auditor | Actions User | Web (reports, dashboards) |
| P4: Safety Manager | Actions User | Web (dashboards) |
| P5: Executive | Actions User | Web (KPI dashboards) |
| P6: Tenant Administrator | Tenant Admin | Web (admin portal) |
| P7: Consulted Worker | Actions User | Web (consultation UI) |
| P8: Automated Service (M2M) | M2M Service | API (client_credentials) |
For full persona definitions including goals, pain points, success criteria,
capabilities, and the persona-module matrix, see the
[canonical persona document](../prd/personas.md).
